answer:
The recommended time window for giving intravenous alteplase in acute ischemic stroke is within 4.5 hours of symptom onset, as per the AHA/ASA Acute Ischemic Stroke Guideline [1]. Mechanical thrombectomy is recommended for large-vessel occlusion within 24 hours in selected patients.

References:
[1] Early Management of Acute Ischemic Stroke, AHA/ASA, 2021.

arguments.query: intravenous alteplase acute ischemic stroke timing

result.query: intravenous alteplase acute ischemic stroke timing

results.content: Intravenous alteplase is recommended within 4.5 hours of symptom onset for eligible patients with acute ischemic stroke. Mechanical thrombectomy is recommended for large-vessel occlusion within 24 hours in selected patients.
results.citation: Early Management of Acute Ischemic Stroke, AHA/ASA, 2021

metadata.title: Early Management of Acute Ischemic Stroke

results.content: For patients with sepsis or septic shock, broad-spectrum antibiotics should be administered within 1 hour of recognition, along with 30 mL/kg of intravenous crystalloid fluid for hypotension or elevated lactate.
results.citation: Management of Sepsis and Septic Shock, Surviving Sepsis Campaign, 2021

metadata.title: Management of Sepsis and Septic Shock

results.content: Amoxicillin is the first-line antibiotic for acute otitis media in children without recent beta-lactam exposure. Watchful waiting may be offered for children 6 months to 12 years with unilateral mild symptoms.
results.source: AAP Clinical Practice Guideline on Acute Otitis Media
results.citation: Diagnosis and Management of Acute Otitis Media, AAP, 2013

metadata.title: Diagnosis and Management of Acute Otitis Media

result.disclaimer: Evidence excerpts for professional review; not a diagnosis.
